word.definition: Cohort refers to a group or band of individuals, as in “a cohort of supporters.” It can also be used for a group of individuals who have a statistical factor (such as age) in common in a demographic study, as in “a cohort of people born in the 1980s.” Cohort can be used for individuals too, as for a friend, companion, or colleague, but it is almost always used in its plural form.

sports_fact.text: Michael Jordan hit .202 in Double-A Birmingham during his 1994 baseball detour — and baseball people respected that it wasn't worse, given he hadn't played since high school.

market.headline: Stock Market Today: Oil Pares Gains as Investors Cling to Hope of Mideast Deal
market.summary: Stocks edge higher to new records

quote.text: This is our purpose: to make as meaningful as possible this life that has been bestowed upon us; to live in such a way that we may be proud of ourselves; to act in such a way that some part of us lives on.
quote.author: Oswald Spengler

history.text: Battle of Monte Porzio: A Roman army supporting Pope Alexander III is defeated by Christian of Buch and Rainald of Dassel.
